description_long: This object is a classic black rotary dial corded telephone, featuring a robust plastic body with a glossy finish. The handset rests securely on a cradle at the top, connected to the base by a coiled cord. The front face displays a circular rotary dial with finger holes for dialing numbers, and the numbers are clearly marked in white. The base is rectangular and slightly tapered, providing stability, while the handset is ergonomically shaped for comfortable use. The coiled cord is flexible and extends from the right side of the base to the handset. This type of phone is reminiscent of mid-20th-century telecommunication devices commonly found in homes and offices.
description: A glossy black rotary dial corded telephone with a coiled cord and a classic ergonomic handset resting on a cradle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, handset on cradle, and coiled cord attached to the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the base and the profile of the handset resting on top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the base and the coiled cord extending outward.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view emphasizing the coiled cord and the handset's position on the cradle.
